Physical Constants#

Physical constants for atmospheric science.

This module defines a collection of widely used physical constants for thermodynamics and atmospheric physics. Each constant is exposed as a simple Python variable, documented with its value, units and scientific meaning. All constants are of float type.

Example

You can import and use the constants directly:

>>> from dephycf import constants as cc
>>> cc.g
9.80665

Here are the main constants defined in dephycf.constants:

Fundamental constants#

g = 9.80665#

Standard gravity acceleration (m s-2).

\[g = 9.80665 \; \text{m s}^{-2}\]
boltzman = 1.380658e-23#

Boltzmann constant (J K-1).

\[k_B = 1.380658 \times 10^{-23} \; \text{J K}^{-1}\]
avogadro = 6.0221367e+23#

Avogadro’s number (mol-1).

\[N_A = 6.0221367 \times 10^{23} \; \text{mol}^{-1}\]
R = 8.3145112119486#

Universal gas constant (J mol-1 K-1).

\[R = N_A \times k_B \approx 8.31451 \; \text{J mol}^{-1} \text{ K}^{-1}\]
sigma = 5.67e-08#

Stefan–Boltzmann constant (W m-2 K-4).

\[\sigma = 5.67 \times 10^{-8} \; \text{W m}^{-2} \text{ K}^{-4}\]

Molecular masses#

Md = 28.9644#

Dry air molar mass (g mol-1).

\[M_d = 28.9644 \; \text{g mol}^{-1}\]
Mv = 18.0153#

Water vapor molar mass (g mol-1).

\[M_v = 18.0153 \; \text{g mol}^{-1}\]
Mo3 = 47.9982#

Ozone molar mass (g mol-1).

\[M_{O_3} = 47.9982 \; \text{g mol}^{-1}\]

Gas constants and heats#

Rd = 287.0596736665907#

Gas constant for dry air (J kg-1 K-1).

\[R_d = 1000. \times \frac{R}{M_d} \approx 287.1 \; \text{g mol}^{-1} \text{ K}^{-1}\]
Rv = 461.5249933083879#

Gas constant for water vapor (J kg-1 K-1).

\[R_v = 1000. \times \frac{R}{M_v} \approx 461.5 \; \text{g mol}^{-1} \text{ K}^{-1}\]
Cpd = 1004.7088578330674#

Dry air specific heat at constant pressure (J kg-1 K-1).

\[C_{pd} = 3.5 \times R_d \approx 1004.7 \; \text{J kg}^{-1} \text{ K}^{-1}\]
Cpv = 1846.0999732335515#

Water vapor specific heat at constant pressure (J kg-1 K-1).

\[C_{pv} = 4 \times R_v \approx 1846.1 \; \text{J kg}^{-1} \text{ K}^{-1}\]

Latent heats at 0°C#

Lv = 2500800.0#

Latent heat of water vaporization (J kg-1).

\[L_v = 2.5008 \times 10^6 \; \text{J kg}^{-1}\]
Ls = 2834500.0#

Latent heat of water sublimation (J kg-1).

\[L_s = 2.8345 \times 10^6 \; \text{J kg}^{-1}\]
Lf = 333700.0#

Latent heat of water fusion (J kg-1).

\[L_f = L_s - L_v = 0.3337 \times 10^6 \; \text{J kg}^{-1}\]

Derived gas constants#

kappa = 0.2857142857142857#

Adiabatic dry air constant (dimensionless).

\[\kappa = \frac{R_d}{C_{pd}} \approx 0.286\]
eps = 0.6219807764013754#

Ratio between dry air and water vapor gas constants (dimensionless).

\[\epsilon = \frac{R_d}{R_v} \approx 0.622\]

Reference values#

p0 = 100000.0#

Reference pressure (Pa).

\[p_0 = 1000 \; \text{hPa}\]
